headline: Amazon’s AWS commits $1 billion toward new unit for embedded AI engineers
summary: Amazon said on Tuesday it is creating a new division under its Amazon Web Services cloud unit employing so-called forward-deployed ‌engineers who embed with customers to help them more quickly and efficiently adopt artificial ‌intelligence software.  The company is committing an initial $1 billion to the initiative with the goal of sending five to six ​pods of engineers to customers for 45-day periods, said Francessca Vasquez, AWS vice president of frontier AI engineering and services.  Forward-deployed engineers are versatile workers ⁠who embed directly alongside clients, navigate internal politics and write production-grade code to help make models deliver results.

raw.headline: Amazon’s AWS commits $1 billion toward new unit for embedded AI engineers
raw.summary: Amazon said on Tuesday it is creating a new division under its Amazon Web Services cloud unit employing so-called forward-deployed ‌engineers who embed with customers to help them more quickly and efficiently adopt artificial ‌intelligence software.  The company is committing an initial $1 billion to the initiative with the goal of sending five to six ​pods of engineers to customers for 45-day periods, said Francessca Vasquez, AWS vice president of frontier AI engineering and services.  Forward-deployed engineers are versatile workers ⁠who embed directly alongside clients, navigate internal politics and write production-grade code to help make models deliver results.

headline: CME Group to Launch Single Stock Futures on July 27

headline: Chip Stocks’ Best Quarter Ever Is Ending With Some Wild Swings
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Chip stocks are heading for their best quarter ever, extending an extraordinary start to the year driven by insatiable demand for artificial intelligence equipment. But after recent jitters sent the stocks tumbling, investors are wondering how much further the rally can go. headline: Big Tech Catches a Bid But Microsoft Is Walking Toward Its Worst Month Since 2008
summary: Wall Street finally exhaled on Monday with a broad tech rebound, but the largest software name in the world refused to come along. Alphabet stepped into the Dow, SpaceX into the Russell, and Rocket Lab paid eight billion dollars to take on a satellite incumbent, a session full of headlines, but only one of them was strange.

headline: Amazon (AMZN) Stock After Recent Pullback Is The Valuation Story Changing?
summary: If you are wondering whether Amazon.com is still reasonably priced after years of attention, the starting point is understanding what the current share price actually reflects. The stock most recently closed at US$240.13, with returns of 3.2% over the past week, a decline of 11.3% over the last month, a 6.0% gain year to date, and returns of 9.5%, 84.4% and 36.8% over the past 1, 3 and 5 years respectively. Recent moves in Amazon.com have been shaped by ongoing discussions about its scale in...

headline: Not Even Musk Has The Answer To AI’s Power Shortage
